Professional Summary

Audit Associate with live experience since January 2024, 14-sector exposure, and strong documentation discipline.

Summary

Professional experience has been built through audit, financial, and consulting assignments performed within NFRS and IFRS reporting contexts. Strengths include complete audit file preparation, evidence organization, software fluency across banking and accounting systems, and reporting support for key management personnel and audit partners.

0.0 Years since January 2024
14 Sectors covered

Professional Focus

Audit execution, complete file preparation, financial review support, and standards-aware work across varied environments.

Current Direction

Targeting banking, finance, and IT opportunities where sector breadth, reporting discipline, and systems exposure are useful immediately.

Core Competencies

Financial statement review Ledger analysis and reconciliations Audit support and file preparation NFRS / IFRS reporting exposure Management and partner reporting

Professional Experience

Experience aligned to audit execution, standards-aware work, and structured reporting.

Experience

PYC and Associates Chartered Accountants

Audit, financial, and consulting exposure

January 2024 to Present

Supported audit, financial, and consulting assignments through evidence review, ledger analysis, file preparation, and structured reporting across multiple sectors.

  • Performed audit planning support, testing, evidence review, ledger analysis, reconciliations, and reporting support aligned with NFRS and IFRS expectations.
  • Prepared complete audit documentation including working papers, evidence files, supporting schedules, and support materials suitable for review.
  • Reported to key management personnel and audit partners while helping review junior work for completeness and clarity.
  • Adapted execution across banking, finance, IT, FDI, manufacturing, education, cooperative, private, listed, consulting, tax, law, costing, and NPO environments.

Software and Systems Exposure

Professional workflow fluency

Ongoing

Built practical software fluency that supports stronger information structure, workflow consistency, and reporting quality in audit and finance work.

  • Worked with confidential proprietary banking systems including CRM, CBS, and HR platforms.
  • Used local tools such as Tally and Rigo to support reporting, ledger work, and documentation-led follow-through.
  • Applied software exposure to keep audit files, evidence structure, and reporting materials organized for review.

Education and Development

Academic grounding aligned with audit, reporting, and business understanding.

Education

Academic pathway

  • BBS Program with continuing focus on financial understanding, reporting context, and business fundamentals.
  • Commitment to disciplined learning that supports practical audit development.

Professional direction

  • Chartered Accountant pathway supported by practical exposure and working-paper discipline.
  • Ongoing development in communication quality, documentation standards, and systems awareness.

Sector Exposure

Industry familiarity developed through 14 sectors of work exposure.

Coverage

Cross-sector exposure has strengthened business-context awareness, procedural adaptability, and communication discipline while maintaining a consistent standard of file quality.
